text: fat is 49.7g. polyunsaturated fat, omega3, and cholesterol are doing most of the work. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
carbs is 23.4g. fibre is where things start to fall off. for a seed, this is a very strong sign.
protein is 17.7g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 2% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 88% DV, calcium at 98% DV, iron at 81% DV stand out most. that is major mineral support.

the upsides first. elite mineral density because the mineral payload is a major category strength beyond a normal seed baseline. easy to use across lots of foods because works well in savoury dishes, baking, and toppings. strong wholefood seed identity because feels like a very legitimate seed-category food rather than an edge case.

now the downsides. contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. not suitable for everyone because sesame allergy risk matters in practice. saturated fat is not negligible because still not a disaster, but it is less clean than the best fat-profile seeds.
B tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
